vehicles.slot: Remove shifter gaiter/boot surround. Flat-bladed screwdriver — pry upward on horizontal dark gray metal hook on lower right of shifter lever to disengage interlock.
shift: Remove the trim surround / gaiter around the gear shifter.
Locate the horizontal dark gray metal hook on the lower-right side of the shifter lever.
Insert flat-bladed screwdriver. Pry upward on the hook to disengage the shift interlock.
While holding the hook up, press brake and move selector to N.
Confirm N. Replace trim.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery is under hood — standard location.
Connect jump pack.
Once powered, shift normally or use gaiter/hook method above.
TRANSFER CASE MUST BE IN NEUTRAL for flatbed, or 2H for wheel-lift.
vehicles.notes: Permanent AWD — transfer case neutral required for flatbed. 4-wheel tow: strict 30 mph / 30 mile limit.
vehicles.warn: Transfer case MUST be in Neutral (flatbed) or 2H equivalent. Permanent AWD — cannot wheel-lift without TC neutral.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED PREFERRED. Flatbed with TC in neutral. Four-wheel-down: TC in neutral, max 30 mph / 30 miles. NEVER wheel-lift front or rear only — permanent AWD will destroy drivetrain.

tc.type: Manual floor lever transfer case (2003-2005) or Electronic rotary dial (2006-2012)
positions: Neutral (N) — REQUIRED for flatbed towing
High Range — normal driving — NOT for towing
Low Range — off-road only — NEVER tow in low range
steps: MANUAL LEVER (2003-2005): Stop completely. Move TC lever to Neutral position.
ELECTRONIC DIAL (2006-2012): Turn dial to Neutral. Wait for N indicator on dash.
Confirm TC Neutral lamp illuminated before loading on flatbed.
IF BATTERY DEAD: Jump battery first. Electronic TC requires power to shift.
If TC cannot be confirmed in Neutral, DO NOT load on flatbed — call for assistance.

towNotes: PERMANENT AWD: never wheel-lift front or rear only — drivetrain destruction guaranteed.
TC must be in Neutral for flatbed loading. Electronic dial requires 12V power.
Air suspension: may have risen/lowered — vehicle height may be non-standard when found.

airSuspension.description: Electronic Air Suspension (EAS) — 4-corner air bags. Provides multiple ride height settings.
towingNotes: Air suspension may fail and leave vehicle at bump stops (very low) or in high position.
If vehicle is in Extended height or failed low, note height for loading approach.
Do NOT attempt to activate air suspension on a dead battery — compressor requires significant power.
Battery jump will allow suspension to normalize — connect jump pack and allow system to stabilize before loading.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard red (+) / black (−) terminals
battery.warnings: TC must be in Neutral for flatbed. Permanent AWD — no wheel-lift.

knownIssues.title: Permanent AWD — Wheel-Lift Will Destroy Drivetrain
knownIssues.detail: The L322 Range Rover has permanent 4WD. You CANNOT use a standard wheel-lift without putting the transfer case in Neutral first. Attempting to tow with only two wheels on the ground while TC is engaged will destroy the transfer case, transmission, and differentials.

knownIssues.title: Air Suspension Failure Common on High-Mileage Units
knownIssues.detail: EAS air suspension failure is extremely common on L322 Range Rovers. Vehicle may be sitting very low (at bump stops) when found. This affects loading angle and clearance. Note: the front air springs and rear air springs fail independently.

vehicles.slot: Remove cupholder inserts from center console. Red nylon release handle underneath. Turn safety lock to unlock position, then pull red handle firmly to engage TC neutral and shift override.
shift: Remove cupholder inserts from center console.
Locate red nylon pull handle underneath cupholders.
Turn the safety lock collar to the UNLOCK position.
Pull the red handle FIRMLY — this disengages the shift interlock.
Press brake. Move the rotary dial selector to N.
Confirm N on instrument cluster.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery under hood — standard location.
Connect jump pack.
Once powered, use rotary dial to select N normally.
Transfer case: must be in Neutral for flatbed (see TC tab).
Red handle method above works without power as emergency backup.
vehicles.notes: Rotary dial shifter. Red handle under cupholders is the manual override. TC must be in Neutral for flatbed.
vehicles.warn: PERMANENT AWD — no wheel-lift without TC neutral. Flatbed strongly preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED STRONGLY PREFERRED with TC in Neutral. Four-wheel-down tow: TC in Neutral, max 30 mph / 30 miles ONLY. NEVER wheel-lift front or rear only.

tc.type: Electronic rotary dial (Terrain Response) — electronic TC
positions: Neutral (N) — REQUIRED for flatbed / four-wheel towing
High Range — NEVER tow in high range
Low Range — NEVER tow in low range
steps: Turn rotary dial to Neutral. Wait for N indicator.
If battery dead: jump first, then shift TC to Neutral via dial.
Confirm Neutral lamp on instrument cluster before loading.
If TC cannot be confirmed in Neutral: DO NOT tow — flatbed with all wheels off ground and TC unknown position will destroy drivetrain.

towNotes: Permanent AWD — no front or rear wheel-lift alone without TC in neutral.
TC electronic — requires 12V power to shift. Jump first if dead.
L405 is very heavy — verify flatbed weight capacity.
30 mph / 30 mile limit strictly enforced for four-wheel tow.

epb.override: Connect 12V to restore power and release EPB via button. Manual: use manual release lever/cable in trunk area if equipped.
steps: Connect jump pack to under-hood 12V terminals.
Restore power. Press and hold EPB button to release.
If manual cable is present in trunk, use it as last resort.
NOTE: Always release EPB before loading on flatbed.

airSuspension.description: Electronic Air Suspension (EAS) — 4-corner. Multiple ride heights. Access Mode lowers vehicle for loading.
towingNotes: Access Mode: press and hold Suspension button with doors open to lower vehicle for easier loading.
If air suspension has failed, vehicle may be at bump stops. Check clearance before loading.
Battery jump allows system to normalize. Allow time for compressor to level vehicle.
Do NOT rock or pitch vehicle aggressively during loading if air suspension is in unusual position.

vehicles.steeringLock: Electronic steering lock. Requires ignition power.

battery.warnings: AGM rated jump pack required. TC must be in Neutral for flatbed. Permanent AWD.

knownIssues.title: Red Handle Is Under the Cupholders — Not at Shifter Base
knownIssues.detail: The emergency shift release on the L405 is a red nylon pull handle UNDERNEATH the cupholder inserts in the center console. You must remove the cupholders to access it. It is not at the base of the rotary selector. Pull firmly after turning the safety lock to unlock.

knownIssues.title: Air Suspension Failure
knownIssues.detail: EAS failures are common on high-mileage L405s. Vehicle may be sitting at bump stops or unevenly when found. This creates unusual loading geometry. A battery jump typically allows the compressor to normalize heights.

vehicles.slot: Red nylon handle under cupholder in center console — same as L405
shift: Remove cupholder from center console.
Locate red nylon handle underneath.
Turn safety lock to UNLOCK. Pull handle firmly.
Press brake. Move J-gate shifter to N.
Confirm N on cluster.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery under hood.
Connect jump pack.
Use red handle method or shift normally once powered.
TC must be in Neutral for flatbed.
vehicles.notes: J-gate shifter. Same red handle override as full-size Range Rover.
vehicles.warn: Permanent AWD — no wheel-lift without TC in neutral.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED with TC in Neutral. Four-wheel-down: TC neutral, max 30 mph / 30 miles.

tc.type: High/Low transfer case with Neutral — electronic shift
positions: Neutral — REQUIRED for flatbed/four-wheel towing
High — NOT for towing
Low — NEVER tow in low range
steps: Select Neutral on the transfer case switch/dial.
Wait for indicator. Confirm neutral before loading.
Battery dead: jump first — TC requires power.

towNotes: Permanent AWD — flatbed with TC in neutral or four-wheel strictly limited.
30 mph / 30 miles for four-wheel tow.
L320 body on frame platform — tow from frame, not body panels.

airSuspension.description: EAS air suspension — same as full Range Rover. Common failure mode.
towingNotes: Same air suspension failure issues as Range Rover L322.
Battery jump to normalize suspension before loading.
Check vehicle height and clearance before approach.

battery.warnings: Permanent AWD — no wheel-lift without TC neutral.

vehicles.slot: Red nylon handle under cupholder inserts in center console
shift: Remove cupholder inserts.
Turn safety lock collar to UNLOCK.
Pull red handle firmly.
Press brake. Use rotary dial to select N.
Confirm N on cluster.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery under hood.
Connect jump pack. Restore power.
Shift TC and gear selector to Neutral normally via rotary dial.
Red handle as backup if electronics fail.
vehicles.notes: Rotary dial shifter. Shares red handle override with L405.
vehicles.warn: Permanent AWD — flatbed with TC neutral required. 30 mph / 30 miles for 4-wheel tow.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED PREFERRED with TC Neutral. Four-wheel-down: TC Neutral, max 30 mph / 30 miles.

positions: Neutral — REQUIRED for flatbed or four-wheel towing
High Range — NOT for towing
Low Range — NEVER tow
steps: Turn rotary dial to Neutral.
Confirm N indicator on dash.
If battery dead: jump first — electronic TC requires power.
No power = no TC shift = FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: Electronic TC requires 12V power — jump first if dead.
30 mph / 30 mile strict limit for four-wheel tow.
Permanent AWD — wheel-lift alone will destroy drivetrain.

epb.override: Restore 12V, release EPB via button. Manual: cable/lever in trunk area.
steps: Jump 12V under-hood terminals.
Press and hold EPB button to release.
If unavailable: locate manual cable in trunk.

airSuspension.description: Electronic Air Suspension. Access Mode available.
towingNotes: Use Access Mode (hold suspension button with doors open) to lower for loading.
Failed suspension common — check vehicle height before approach.
Battery jump allows compressor to normalize.

battery.warnings: AGM battery — use AGM-rated jump pack. TC neutral required for flatbed.

vehicles.slot: Electronic rotary dial shifter — override slot near rotary dial base, or press/hold brake + start button sequence
shift: Press and hold brake pedal.
Press start button (without foot on brake pedal first) to enter accessory mode.
Then hold brake and press the + gear paddle or rotary to move to N.
Alternative: locate override slot near rotary dial base. Insert key, engage, move to N.
Note: auxiliary battery under passenger glove box foot well — may need this for power.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Main battery under hood OR auxiliary battery under passenger glove box foot well.
If main battery dead, auxiliary may still have power — check under glove box.
Connect jump pack to under-hood terminals.
Restore power. Use rotary dial to select N.
AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.notes: Auxiliary battery may be under passenger glove box for safety/electronic systems. Check both locations.
vehicles.warn: AWD: flatbed preferred. Check for auxiliary battery under glove box foot well.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: rear lift (front drive wheels roll). AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: Auxiliary battery under glove box foot well — useful if main battery is dead.
AWD Haldex: flatbed preferred.
PHEV (2021+): 12V auxiliary only — never touch HV system.

epb.override: Restore 12V (check auxiliary under glove box), then EPB button release.
steps: Check for auxiliary battery under passenger glove box foot well.
If present and charged, use it to power system.
Press and hold EPB button to release.
Otherwise: jump from under-hood terminals.

battery.location: Under hood (main). Auxiliary battery possibly under passenger glove box foot well.
battery.warnings: Check for auxiliary battery under glove box. PHEV: 12V only.

knownIssues.title: Auxiliary Battery Under Glove Box
knownIssues.detail: The Evoque has a small auxiliary battery under the passenger-side glove box foot area. This battery powers the rotary selector safety system. If the main battery is dead but this aux battery has charge, you may be able to use it to shift into Neutral without a full jump.

vehicles.slot: Red nylon handle under cupholder inserts in center console
shift: Remove cupholder inserts from center console.
Locate red nylon handle underneath.
Turn safety lock to UNLOCK. Pull handle firmly.
Press brake. Move J-gate shifter to N.
Confirm N on instrument cluster.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery under hood.
Connect jump pack.
Use rotary/J-gate to shift once powered, or red handle method.
TC must be in Neutral for flatbed.
vehicles.notes: LR4 name used in US market. Same J-gate/red handle system as Sport.
vehicles.warn: Permanent AWD — no wheel-lift without TC neutral. Heavy vehicle.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED with TC in Neutral. Four-wheel-down: TC Neutral, max 30 mph / 30 miles.

tc.type: Electronic transfer case with High, Low, and Neutral
positions: Neutral — REQUIRED for flatbed / four-wheel towing
High Range — NOT for towing
Low Range — NEVER tow in low
steps: Select Neutral on TC switch.
Wait for N indicator. Confirm before loading.
Battery dead: jump first. TC electronic — requires power.

towNotes: Very heavy vehicle — confirm flatbed capacity.
Electronic TC requires power — jump first if dead.
Shift lock solenoid problems reported on some year models.

airSuspension.description: Electronic Air Suspension — 4-corner. Common failure mode.
towingNotes: EAS failure common. Vehicle may sit very low or unevenly.
Jump battery to normalize suspension before loading.
Check clearances before approach.

battery.warnings: Permanent AWD — TC neutral required. Heavy — check flatbed capacity.

knownIssues.title: Shift Lock Solenoid Failures
knownIssues.detail: Some LR4 model years have documented shift lock solenoid failures that prevent the shift interlock from releasing even with the override method. If the red handle method fails and the vehicle has power, a failing solenoid may be the cause. Flatbed with TC in neutral is the safest fallback.

vehicles.slot: Electronic shifter with mechanical override — override slot near base of rotary/lever selector under console trim
shift: Locate mechanical override slot near selector base under console trim.
Insert key or flat tool. Press override plunger.
Press brake. Move selector to N.
Electronic override: rotary dial to N if power available.
PHEV: select N on selector or touchscreen if available.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery under hood.
Connect jump pack.
Restore power. Use selector normally.
TC: must be in Neutral for flatbed — electronic TC requires power.
PHEV: 12V only — never touch HV system.
vehicles.notes: Modern fully electronic vehicle. PHEV (P400e) has HV system — orange cables.
vehicles.warn: Permanent AWD — TC neutral required. PHEV: never touch HV system. Flatbed strongly preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED STRONGLY PREFERRED with TC in Neutral. Permanent AWD — no wheel-lift without TC neutral.

tc.type: Electronic transfer case with Terrain Response 2 — Neutral selection via dial
positions: Neutral — REQUIRED for flatbed towing
High Range — NOT for towing
Low Range — NEVER tow in low
steps: Select Neutral via TC rotary or Terrain Response dial.
Confirm N indicator before loading.
Battery dead: jump 12V first — TC is fully electronic.
PHEV: 12V auxiliary only for jumping.

towNotes: Modern L663 is fully electronic — all systems require 12V power.
PHEV: HV system present — orange cables. 12V auxiliary jump only.
Very wide vehicle — measure before using narrow flatbeds.
Limited public documentation on emergency procedures — Land Rover assist recommended.

epb.override: Restore 12V, EPB button release. Manual access requires wheel removal for caliper access.
steps: Connect jump pack to 12V under-hood terminals.
Press and hold EPB button to release.
If power not available: requires wheel removal and caliper tool for manual retraction.

airSuspension.description: Electronic Air Suspension standard or optional. Multiple ride height modes. Access Height available.
towingNotes: Use Access Height (via terrain response controls) to lower vehicle for loading.
PHEV variant is heavier — factor in battery weight for loading.
If suspension failed: battery jump to normalize. Allow time for compressor.

vehicles.steeringLock: Electronic steering lock — requires ignition power.

battery.warnings: AGM battery — use AGM-rated pack. Permanent AWD — TC neutral required. PHEV: 12V only.

vehicles.slot: Console-mounted selector — override slot or mechanical release at selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
AUTOMATIC: locate override slot or mechanical release near selector base on console.
Insert flathead screwdriver into slot and press/lever the interlock cam upward.
While holding, press brake. Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL: depress clutch fully and move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery under the hood — standard location. Connect jump pack.
Once powered, shift normally.
PERMANENT 4WD — transfer case must be in Neutral for flatbed or four-wheel-down towing.
Manual TC lever: stop completely, move TC to Neutral position.
NEVER wheel-lift front or rear only — permanent AWD will destroy drivetrain.
vehicles.notes: Permanent 4WD. TC must be in Neutral for any towing. Manual TC lever.
vehicles.warn: PERMANENT AWD — NEVER wheel-lift front or rear only. TC must be in Neutral.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED with TC in Neutral. Four-wheel-down: TC in Neutral, max 30 mph / 30 miles. NEVER wheel-lift front or rear alone.

positions: Neutral (N) — REQUIRED for towing
High Range — normal driving
Low Range — NEVER tow in low
steps: Stop completely.
Move TC floor lever to Neutral.
Confirm Neutral before loading or towing.

towNotes: Permanent AWD — TC must be in Neutral for any towing.
Never wheel-lift front or rear alone.
Manual TC lever — stop completely before shifting TC.

battery.warnings: Permanent AWD — never wheel-lift without TC in Neutral.

knownIssues.title: Permanent AWD — Wheel-Lift Will Destroy Drivetrain
knownIssues.detail: The Range Rover Classic has permanent 4WD with no 2WD mode. You CANNOT wheel-lift from either end without first putting the transfer case in Neutral. Attempting to tow with only two wheels off the ground while TC is engaged will destroy the transfer case and differentials.

vehicles.slot: Console-mounted selector — override slot at selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
AUTOMATIC: locate the override slot at the selector base on the center console.
Insert a flathead screwdriver or key and press the interlock cam to release.
While holding, press brake. Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL: depress clutch fully and move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery under the hood — connect jump pack.
Once powered, shift normally.
PERMANENT AWD — TC must be in Neutral for flatbed or four-wheel-down towing.
Manual TC floor lever: stop completely, shift to Neutral.
NEVER wheel-lift front or rear only.
vehicles.notes: Permanent AWD. Manual TC floor lever. Same rules as Range Rover Classic.
vehicles.warn: PERMANENT AWD — NEVER wheel-lift front or rear alone. TC to Neutral before any towing.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED with TC in Neutral. Four-wheel-down: TC Neutral, max 30 mph / 30 miles. NEVER wheel-lift.

positions: Neutral — REQUIRED for towing
High Range
Low Range — NEVER tow in low
steps: Stop completely.
Move TC floor lever to Neutral.
Confirm Neutral before loading.

towNotes: Permanent AWD — TC to Neutral before towing.
Never wheel-lift front or rear alone.
Stop completely before shifting TC lever.

battery.warnings: Permanent AWD — TC Neutral required for any towing.

vehicles.slot: Console-mounted selector — override slot near selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON (key to Run position — do not start).
Locate the shift lock override: cap or slot near selector base on center console.
Pry off the plastic cap with your ignition key or a small flathead screwdriver.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery under the hood — connect jump pack.
Once powered, press brake and shift normally.
FLATBED ONLY — Freelander Haldex AWD cannot be disabled. No 2WD mode.
MANUAL: depress clutch and move to Neutral. FLATBED ONLY — no 2WD.
vehicles.notes: Haldex AWD — FLATBED ONLY. No 2WD mode on any Freelander trim.
vehicles.warn: FLATBED ONLY — Haldex AWD cannot be disabled. Never wheel-lift.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — permanent Haldex AWD with no 2WD mode.
towNotes: Haldex AWD — flatbed only. No 2WD mode.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: Permanent Haldex AWD. No traditional transfer case lever. Flatbed preferred.
vehicles.warn: AWD — flatbed preferred. No transfer case to disengage.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED PREFERRED — permanent AWD cannot be disengaged. Wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle if no flatbed.
towNotes: Permanent Haldex AWD — cannot disengage. Flatbed is safest.
Wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle if flatbed unavailable.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
Transfer case must be in High mode for road towing — do NOT leave in Low.
vehicles.notes: Full-time 4WD with electronic TC. Must shift TC to high range before road towing.
vehicles.warn: Permanent 4WD — flatbed preferred. If wheel-lift, must use dollies on free-rolling axle.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED PREFERRED. Wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle if no flatbed.

tc.type: Electronic rotary dial on center console
positions: High — standard road range — required for road towing
Low — low range — NEVER tow in low range on pavement
steps: Ensure TC is in High range (not Low) before road towing.
TC is full-time — cannot disengage to 2WD.
FLATBED is safest option.
IF wheel-lift: dollies MUST be under the free-rolling axle.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first, confirm TC in High, then proceed.

towNotes: Full-time permanent 4WD. FLATBED is safest.
If wheel-lift must be used: dollies REQUIRED on free-rolling axle.
DEAD BATTERY: Jump first before any tow movement.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — touch-sensitive panel on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
The Velar uses a touch-sensitive electronic gear selector (Touch Pro Duo) — no physical lever.
With power on, press N on the touch panel to select Neutral.
If power is lost: connect jump pack to 12V battery. Battery is under the hood.
Once powered, use the touch panel to select N.
Electronic selectors require power — no mechanical override slot.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, use the touch panel to select N.
FLATBED preferred — permanent AWD.
vehicles.notes: Fully electronic touch panel selector — no physical gear lever. Permanent AWD. Flatbed preferred.
vehicles.warn: Permanent AWD — flatbed preferred. Electronic selector requires power.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED PREFERRED — permanent AWD. Wheel-lift with dollies if no flatbed.

tc.type: Electronic — managed by Terrain Response system
positions: High — standard range — for road towing
Low — NEVER tow on pavement in low range
steps: Ensure Terrain Response is not in Low range before road towing.
Full-time permanent AWD — cannot disengage to 2WD.
FLATBED is safest.
IF wheel-lift: dollies on free-rolling axle REQUIRED.

towNotes: Full-time permanent AWD. FLATBED strongly preferred.
Electronic touch panel requires power to shift.

knownIssues.title: Fully Electronic Touch Panel — No Physical Selector
knownIssues.detail: The Velar has a Touch Pro Duo glass-panel selector system. There is no physical gear lever or traditional shift lock slot. Without 12V power, the selector cannot be operated. Always restore 12V power before attempting to select Neutral.

battery.warnings: AGM battery — use AGM-compatible jump pack. Electronic selector requires 12V power.

vehicles.slot: Electronic rotary selector rises from console — slot near selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
The Discovery 5 uses an electronic rotary 'rising' selector on the center console.
With power on, rotate selector to N (Neutral).
If power is lost: connect jump pack to 12V battery under the hood.
Once powered, rotate the selector to N.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, rotate the electronic selector to N.
FLATBED preferred — permanent AWD.
vehicles.notes: Electronic rotary selector. Permanent AWD. Must have power for selector. Flatbed preferred.
vehicles.warn: Permanent AWD — flatbed preferred. Electronic rotary requires power.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED PREFERRED. Wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle if no flatbed.

positions: High — standard range — required for road towing
Low — NEVER tow on pavement in low range
steps: Ensure TC is in High range before road towing.
Full-time AWD — cannot disengage.
FLATBED is safest.
IF wheel-lift: dollies REQUIRED on free-rolling axle.

towNotes: Full-time permanent AWD. FLATBED strongly preferred.
Electronic rotary selector requires 12V power to operate.

battery.warnings: AGM battery. Electronic selector requires power.
